heroHeadline: Iran Opens Hormuz; Trump Maintains Naval Blockade Pending Peace Deal
heroSubtitle: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' on April 17, prompting a 10%+ drop in oil prices — but Trump immediately clarified the US naval blockade 'will remain in full force' until a comprehensive long-term peace agreement is reached. An April 22 ceasefire renewal deadline looms as the next critical inflection point. Meanwhile, new polling shows Trump's economic approval fell to a career low of 31%, four Republican House members broke with Trump to protect Haitian deportation protections, and a growing immigration application backlog of 2 million+ cases leaves millions vulnerable to deportation.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model.
